english: walk, kyeu (kye'u) _gro ('gro) mi (mi) nús-pa (nus pa) góg-pa (gog pa) tsam (tsam) an infant, a child, that creeps only, and is not yet able to walk Dzl.; _gró-ba ('gro ba) dań (dang) nyál-ba (nyal ba) dań (dang) _dúg-pa ('dug pa) the walking, lying and sitting Dzl.; com. in a more gen. sense: to go, to move, _gró-am ('gro 'am) mi (mi) _gro ('gro) will you go or not? rgyál-poi (rgyal po'i) mdún-la (mdun la) _gró-bai ('gro ba'i) lág-ca (lag ca) the things going, i.e. carried, before the king Glr.; to go away, da (da) ńa (nga) _gró-bar ('gro bar) żu (zhu) now I beg to be permitted to go Pth.; ynás-nas (gnas nas) _gró-ba ('gro ba) to go away from a place, to leave, k'yím-nas (khyim nas) _gro ('gro) mi (mi) p'ód-na (phod na) if one cannot leave his house, part from home Pth.; to go out, k'yod (khyod) nyín-par (nyin par) rtág-tu (rtag tu) _gró-na ('gro na) if during the day you always go out, are from home Dzl.; to travel, p'an-ts'ún-du (phan tshun du) _gró-ba ('gro ba) Dzl., p'ar (phar) _gro ('gro) ts'ur (tshur) _gró-ba ('gro ba) Pth. travelling there and back; yar (yar) _gro ('gro) mar (mar) _gró-ba ('gro ba) to travel up and down, up hill and down hill Glr.; _gró-c'os-su ('gro chos su) as a spiritual vademecum Mil.; _gró-tse ('gro tse) on the way, on the road; opp. to _óń-ba ('ong ba) (more fully: p'ar (phar) _gro ('gro) ts'ur (tshur) _oń-ba ('ong ba), col. yoń-ba (yong ba)) to go and to come back; hence _gro-tse ('gro tse) may also mean: on the way thither; _gro-_oń-méd-pa ('gro 'ong med pa) a thing that is neither going nor coming, but always remaining in its place Mil.; to go, move on, to continue one's way, esp. in the phrase soń(-soń)-ba-las (song(-song) ba las). Connected with adverbs and postpositions: p'yír (phyir) _gró-ba ('gro ba) to return, go home, to come back, also: to go out, mdún-du (mdun du), sńón-du (sngon du), sńún-la (sngun la) _gró-ba ('gro ba) to go before, pass before, precede (mdún-du (mdun du) referring to space only, sńón-du (sngon du) and sńún-du (sngun du) both to space and time); rjés-su (rjes su) _gró-ba ('gro ba) to follow, come after or later, to succeed, also to give one's self over to, to addict one's self to (e.g. ill courses) Ld.-Glr. Schl. p. 7, b; _gro ('gro) cug (cug), C. let it be, let it take its course! — rkyál-_gro (rkyal 'gro) a swimming fish Cs. — dgúr (dgur) (or rgur (rgur))-_gro ('gro) = dud-_gro (dud 'gro). — grúl-_gro (grul 'gro) pacing, walking Cs. — rgyúg-_gro (rgyug 'gro) running, galloping Cs. — ńán-_gro (ngan 'gro) going to damnation, ńán-soń (ngan song) having gone to damnation, ńán-soń (ngan song) ysum (gsum) the three damned, or not saved, classes of beings (v. sub II); ńán-soń (ngan song) ysum (gsum) is opposed to bdé-_gro (bde 'gro), and often used in a general sense = 'hell'. — sńón-_gro (sngon 'gro) 1. preceding, foregoing, previous, former. 2. preface, introduction, opp. to dńos-gżi (dngos gzhi), the thing itself, the text etc. Thgr. and elsewh. — c'os (chos) t'ams-cád-kyi (thams cad kyi) sńón-_groi (sngon 'gro'i) sláb-bya (slab bya) Petersb. Verzeichniss no. 409) does not mean: 'advice given by the former (teachers)' Sch., but: introductory and preparatory doctrines. — mc'oń-_gro (mchong 'gro) (going in leaps) a frog Cs. — nyál-_gro (nyal 'gro) (creeping, crawling) a worm. — ltó-_gro (lto 'gro) (crawling, sliding on the belly) worm, snake, frq. — dúd-_gro (dud 'gro) (Ssk., तिर्यंच्) walking in an inclined posture, an animal, v. sub. II. — bdé-_gro (bde 'gro) going to happiness: the happy, the blessed, also bdér-_gro (bder 'gro) Was., opp. to ńán-_gro (ngan 'gro), v. above; usually in a gen. sense, like our 'heaven'; bdé-_gro (bde 'gro) mt'o-rís-kyi (mtho ris kyi) lus (lus) t'ób-pa (thob pa) to receive a heavenly, glorified body, v. lus (lus). — _dúr-_gro ('dur 'gro) trotting Cs. — p'ur-gro (phur 'gro) a bird Cs. — p'yé-_gro (phye 'gro) = ltó-_gro (lto 'gro). — láńs-_gro (langs 'gro) walking erect, man. — 2. to get, to get into, to enter c'i-bdag-gi ('chi bdag gi) dbáń-du (dbang du) sóń-ba (song ba) having got into the power of death S.g.; grál-gyi (gral gyi) t'á-mar (tha mar) soń (song) they got (in a miraculous manner) to the end Dzl. VS, 4. b.; de (de) nyid (nyid) míg-tu (mig tu) sóń-na (song na) if the same (a little hair) gets into the eye Thgy. — 3. to find room in, to be contained in, like sóń-ba (song ba): til-rkyáń (til rkyang) k'al (khal) brgyád-cu (brgyad cu) _gró-ba ('gro ba) żig (zhig) a sesame store-room that will hold 80 bushels; k'al (khal) ycíg (gcig) _gró-bai ('gro ba'i) żiń (zhing) as much land as can be sown with a bushel of corn (prop. a field holding a bushel) Pth. — 4. to turn to, to be trans-formed into, syn. to gyúr-ba (gyur ba) and often used instead of it, but only in more re-cent writings, and in the col. language of C. (in W. *c'á-c'e* (*cha che*) is much more in use): dúg-tu (dug tu) _gro ('gro) it turns to poison, it is changed into poison Mil.; k'yi-mo (khyi mo) żig-tu (zhig tu) soń (song) she was changed into a bitch Mil.; mt'ar (mthar) gyúr-nas (gyur nas) sdug-bsńál-du (sdug bsngal du) _gró-bas-na ('gro bas na) because they finally -change and are turned into misery Thgy.; lóg-par (log par) _gró-ba ('gro ba) to take an un-favourable turn, to have a fatal issue (of a cure) Lt.; da (da) sgrúb-ynas-su (sgrub gnas su) soń (song) yod (yod) it has now become a place of meditation, it has been transformed into sacred ground
